solution: Using \( b^{m}\cdot b^{n} = b^{m+n} \) and \( b^{m}/b^{n} = b^{m-n} \), the expression equals \( [[b]]^{[[e1]] + [[e2]] - [[e3]]} = [[b]]^{[[E]]} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This multiplies the base by the combined exponent instead of raising the base to that power.
This reports the exponent \( [[E]] \) itself instead of \( [[b]]^{[[E]]} \).
This uses an exponent one too large.
This adds all three exponents instead of subtracting the last one.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by two from the correct value.

solution: By the power-of-a-power rule, \( (b^{m})^{n} = b^{mn} \), so this equals \( [[b]]^{[[e1]] \cdot [[e2]]} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This adds the exponents, as when multiplying two powers, rather than multiplying them.
This multiplies the base by both exponents instead of raising it to their product.
This uses an exponent one too large on the product.
This reports the product of the exponents itself, not the base raised to that product.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

solution: A negative exponent means take the reciprocal: \( [[b]]^{-[[n]]} = \dfrac{1}{[[b]]^{[[n]]}} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This negates the value instead of taking its reciprocal.
This drops the negative sign, giving a positive power instead of its reciprocal.
This takes the reciprocal but keeps an incorrect negative sign.
This multiplies the base by the exponent in the denominator rather than raising the base to a power.
This is double the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

solution: A \( 1/n \) power is the same as an \( n \)-th root. Since \( [[b]] = [[k]]^{[[n]]} \), \( [[b]]^{1/[[n]]} = [[k]] = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This divides by \( n \) instead of taking the \( n \)-th root.
This subtracts \( n \) instead of taking the \( n \)-th root.
This uses an exponent one too small on \( k \) when recovering the base.
This swaps the roles of the base and the root index.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This drops the negative sign, giving a positive power of \( k \) instead of its reciprocal.
This has the correct reciprocal magnitude but an incorrect negative sign in front.
This inverts the fractional exponent, using \( n/m \) instead of \( m/n \).
This takes the reciprocal of \( b \) itself instead of \( k^{m} \).
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

solution: Since the exponent \( 2[[n]] \) is even and \( [[b]] > 0 \), \( \sqrt{[[b]]^{2[[n]]}} = [[b]]^{[[n]]} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This halves the whole power instead of halving just the exponent.
This leaves the expression unrooted, forgetting the square root entirely.
This uses an exponent one too small.
This doubles the correctly-rooted value for no valid reason.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

solution: This is a difference of squares, \( (\sqrt{[[a]]}+[[c]])(\sqrt{[[a]]}-[[c]]) = (\sqrt{[[a]]})^{2} - [[c]]^{2} = [[a]] - [[c]]^{2} = [[answer]] \), so the radical always cancels out cleanly.
distractor_rationales: This adds \( c^{2} \) instead of subtracting it.
This flips the sign of the correct difference of squares.
This leaves a radical in the answer, forgetting the two terms multiply to a clean integer.
This forgets to square \( c \) before subtracting.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

solution: Since \( \sqrt{[[a]]}\cdot\sqrt{[[b]]} = \sqrt{[[a]][[b]]} \) and \( [[a]][[b]] = [[c]]^{2}[[d]]^{2}[[e]]^{2} \) is a perfect square, this simplifies to \( [[c]]\cdot[[d]]\cdot[[e]] = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This adds the two radicands instead of multiplying the radicals.
This takes the square root of the SUM of the radicands rather than the product.
This multiplies the three factors but adds an extra 1.
This multiplies the two radicands directly instead of taking the square root of their product.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
